input.headline: NASA unveils close-up pictures of the comet popping by from another star

raw.headline: NASA unveils close-up pictures of the comet popping by from another star

winner: NASA finally caught a high-definition photo of the comet, and it turns out the "space rock" is actually just a very large, very lonely potato that failed its audition for a stew.

candidates.text: NASA just released photos of a comet that popped by from another star — turns out, it was just looking for a place to park its interstellar RV.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific metaphor with a touch of whimsy, aligns well with the headline, and uses natural language effectively."}

candidates.text: NASA says the comet took a selfie before leaving — and it looks like it’s been to a spa.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'selfie' and 'spa' add humor with a touch of whimsy, aligning well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: NASA finally caught a high-definition photo of the comet, and it turns out the "space rock" is actually just a very large, very lonely potato that failed its audition for a stew.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison of a comet to a potato adds humor with a touch of absurdity, while maintaining brevity and natural language."}

candidates.text: NASA says the comet just popped by, leaving no forwarding address.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'popped by' and 'no forwarding address' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: I guess that comet was a little *interstellar* with its visit!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7, "reason": "The joke is clever with the wordplay on 'interstellar,' but it's slightly generic and lacks the specificity that could make it more surprising or impactful."}

candidates.text: Astronomers were thrilled when they got a glimpse of that passing comet—turns out, it was just a space rock with a really expensive viewfinder.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the pun on 'viewfinder' feels a bit forced and less natural. It also doesn't fully tie into the headline's emphasis on NASA and the comet being from another star."}

candidates.text: NASA released close-ups of a comet that popped by from another star — turns out, it was just a stellar breeze.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and uses a play on 'stellar breeze,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the specificity and punch needed for maximum humor."}

candidates.text: NASA’s high-definition photos of the comet were so detailed that they actually managed to capture the comet looking embarrassed to be caught in its underwear.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the 'embarrassed' twist, but the metaphor of a comet being caught in its underwear feels forced and lacks natural flow. It's specific to the headline but misses some punch."}

candidates.text: NASA's new photos of the comet from another star prove that even celestial bodies can have a real meltdown when they're under pressure.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the 'meltdown' pun, but it's a bit generic and doesn't fully tie into the specific context of the comet popping by from another star."}
